---
title: "AI-Powered CV Updater"
description: "A tool that takes a job posting and automatically updates a LaTeX CV using a language model, reducing to seconds what otherwise takes hours."
date: "2025-12-01"
categories: [NLP, LLMs, Automation, Python, LaTeX]
image: ../assets/cv-updater-preview.svg
---

## The Problem

Tailoring a CV to each job posting is tedious but important: ATS systems and recruiters prioritize candidates whose profile closely mirrors the keywords in the job description. This manual process can take 30–60 minutes per application.

---

## The Solution

A Python tool that:

1. **Reads the job posting** (pasted text or URL)
2. **Analyzes the base CV** in LaTeX format
3. **Calls an LLM** to identify which experiences and skills to highlight
4. **Rewrites the relevant sections** of the CV and generates a new `.tex` file ready to compile

---

## Demo

> ⚠️ *Add a GIF or screenshot showing the flow: job input → generated CV.*

---

## Tech Stack

| Component | Technology |
|-----------|-----------|
| Language | Python |
| LLM | <!-- API used: OpenAI, Anthropic, Groq, etc. --> |
| Output format | LaTeX |
| Interface | <!-- CLI / Streamlit / other --> |

---

## Architecture

```
job_posting.txt
       │
       ▼
  [Job parser]  ──→  keywords, required skills
       │
       ▼
  [LLM Prompt]  ──→  instruction + base CV + keywords
       │
       ▼
  [Post-processing]  ──→  replaces sections in the .tex
       │
       ▼
  cv_adapted.tex  ──→  compile with pdflatex
```

---

## Usage Example

> ⚠️ *Add a real example here.*

```bash
python update_cv.py --job posting.txt --cv base_cv.tex --output adapted_cv.tex
```

---

## Results

- ⏱️ Adaptation time: from ~45 min to ~30 seconds
- ✅ Preserves the original LaTeX format and style
- 🎯 Automatically identifies the most relevant sections for each posting

---

## Code

🔗 [github.com/YeisonBL/cv-actualizador-ia](https://github.com/YeisonBL/cv-actualizador-ia)

---

## Learnings

> ⚠️ *Add a reflection on the process, limitations, or next steps.*
